question.text: Scenario: Mr. Rahul Mehta, aged 35, earns Rs. 1,20,000 per month (net take-home). He has the following outstanding debts: (1) Home loan: Rs. 35 lakh outstanding at 8.5% p.a., EMI Rs. 32,000, remaining tenure 15 years; (2) Car loan: Rs. 4,50,000 outstanding at 11% p.a., EMI Rs. 10,000, remaining tenure 5 years; (3) Personal loan: Rs. 2,00,000 outstanding at 16% p.a., EMI Rs. 6,500, remaining tenure 3 years; (4) Credit card outstanding: Rs. 80,000 at 42% p.a. (revolving credit), minimum payment Rs. 4,000/month. His total monthly expenses (excluding debt servicing) are Rs. 45,000. He has recently received an annual bonus of Rs. 3,00,000 and wants advice on how to deploy it. His savings bank balance is Rs. 1,50,000 and he has Rs. 2,00,000 in a liquid mutual fund. Question 4 of 5: After clearing the credit card and personal loan, Rahul has Rs. 20,000 remaining from the bonus. His financial adviser should recommend:

answer.text: Investing it all in equity mutual funds immediately
Adding it to the emergency fund in a liquid asset, as his current emergency fund covers only about 2.6 months of expenses
Making a prepayment on the home loan
Taking a foreign vacation to celebrate being debt-lighter

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Current emergency fund = Savings bank Rs. 1,50,000 + Liquid MF Rs. 2,00,000 = Rs. 3,50,000. Monthly expenses (non-debt) = Rs. 45,000 + debt servicing Rs. 42,000 = Rs. 87,000. Emergency fund covers 3,50,000/87,000 = 4.02 months. Adding Rs. 20,000 brings it to Rs. 3,70,000 (4.25 months). Still below the recommended 6 months. Building emergency fund should take priority. **The Trap:** Equity investment sounds tempting but the emergency fund is insufficient. Rs. 20,000 prepayment on a Rs. 35 lakh home loan has minimal impact. **Cross-Exam Context:** Emergency fund adequacy assessment is tested in X-A (Ch 3). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Emergency fund first, debt reduction second, investments third — this is the correct financial planning hierarchy for someone with a thin liquidity cushion.
